source_pr.title: executor: copy row data instead of refereeing chunk.Row in some window functions.
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

Fixes #11614, and fixes #11626 .
If a `chunk.Chunk` reuse into a window function executor, it may break the internal memory reference in function `RANK` or `PERCENT_RANK`.

1. Father calls `chk := NewChunk()`
2. Father calls `windowExecutor.Next(chk)`
3. The `windowExecutor` put column results into `chk` while refereeing the `chunk.Row` into [`PartialResult`](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/f98ef22/executor/aggfuncs/aggfuncs.go#L89) of  function `RANK` or `PERCENT_RANK`.
4. Father calls `windowExecutor.Next(chk)` again to collect next batch of result and reuses the same `chk`.
5. `chunk.Row` refereced by `PartialResult` is broken when `chk.Reset` is called.

- [x] TODO: fix the other window functions which may lead this problem.

### What is changed and how it works?

Change step 3 above, copy row data instead of refereeing chunk.Row in window functions which rely on data comparing.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Code changes

- None

Side effects

 - Possible performance regression

* note: no func `lag` because it cannot be finished with old code.

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to timing-sensitive asynchronous behavior (background tasks/events not fully settled). This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
